rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TONI MARIE MOORE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The attorney's declaration filed on 9/29/23 (but not e-filed until 10/2/23) AMENDED the petition. An amending pleading should be appropriately (correctly) captioned as such and requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) A court-appointed fiduciary is required to sign and verify her own pleadings. [Probate Code Section 1023 and CRC 7.103(c).] (3) It does not appear that all receipts have been filed yet for the tangible personal property already distributed. (4) Losses on sales were reported to be $139,565.15 on Exhibit G to the petition. Paragraph 21 of the petition showed the total as $139,299.72. The actual total of the individual losses listed in the chart in paragraph 21 of the petition appears to be $139,542.15. How did the administrator arrive at $141,599.74 total losses in the new Exhibit G on page 6 of the attorney's declaration when including the $2,300.00 value of the tangible personal property disposed of? (5) Why was the value of the tangible personal property distributed to Patricia Toney ($200 for silver wedding bands and $600 for the coin collection), reportedly community property, not equalized in Exhibit C of the attorney's declaration? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 11/21/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The incorrect proposed distributions shown in the "declaration" was not just a scrivener's error; the correction was a further amendment. However, since no notice was given for the "declaration", and the above issues will be addressed in a proper AMENDMENT to the petition and notice given for the AMENDMENT after it is filed, this further amendment will be overlooked for now. A pleading should stand on its own and not rely on prior reports or "respond" to issues raised by an examiner.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KAR WAI LEUNG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/4/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The "supplemental to first and final report," etc., filed on 7/27/23 AMENDED the petition. The caption was misleading and notice of the "supplemental" pleading was mailed on 7/27/23 for a hearing on 8/7/23, which was not sufficient notice. (2) Paragraph 7 of the petition has not yet been corrected. (3) The number of shares of the Schwab Total Stock Market Index fund appears to be 2,636.301, not 2,636.3301. (4) The number of shares of several of the assets differs from the shares on the I&A. Paragraph 12 of the petition was incomplete. (Transferring cash is also NOT a change in the form of assets. [Probate Code Section 1063(b).] Selling of shares of securities IS a change in the form of assets and must be reported even when the accounting has been waived. [CRC 7.550(b).] (5) The attorney declaration filed on 8/16/23 (no courtesy copy) does not meet the requirements of CRC 7.702. Furthermore, there are references to an "assistant", but the time sheets are not clear as to who is billing what hours or what the billing party's hourly rate is and if the "assistant" qualifies to bill. (Is s/he an associate attorney or a paralegal?) (6) Is the administrator no longer seeking to be reimbursed $13,072.71 for funeral expenses? (7) The second $435 filing fee for this petition will not be allowed. If the attorney insists on being reimbursed this second filing fee, an appearance will be required after all remaining issues are addressed. Deadline for providing courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues is 11/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (Market values are meaningless for proposed distribution purposes, as they fluctuate. SHARES of securities are what the Court will order to be distributed.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH KIRN BROZELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 11/08/2023, as nothing has been filed to address the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 08/09/2023): 1) Did the decedent marry Mark Rowen after executing the will? If so, does Mark Rowen qualify as an omitted spouse under Probate Code section 21610? 2) Please provide facts in support of the separate property characterization of each asset of the estate. Please provide a copy of the vesting deed if available. 3) The decedent died more than two years ago. Does the petitioner anticipate obtaining waivers of account from all the beneficiaries? See LRSF 14.34(A). 4) Re the sale of the decedent's vehicle, the information required by CRC 7.250(a)(3)-(5) must be provided. 5) Allegations must be made as to whether there are any assignments or powers of attorney that may affect distribution, as required by LRSF 14.35(J)(1). 6) The assets on hand do not appear to include "personal property' listed on the inventory and appraisal (item 1 of Attachment 2). 7) The petition must be amended and renoticed to propose a specific period for which the estate administration is sought to be continued. An appearance may be required on 11/08/2023, if no satisfactory progress is shown to address the issues.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CAROL BARBARA HICKS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 07/12/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 09/13/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) Is the estate property the decedent's separate property, her one-half share of community property, or both? 3) The cash on hand is exactly the same as the inventory and appraisal value. Has the petitioner not opened an interest-bearing account under the name of the estate, as required by Probate Code section 9652? 4) The cash on hand exceeds the FDIC limit. A proof must be filed to confirm that the uninsured portion has been transferred to at least two different financial institutions in observance of the FDIC limit or has been invested as authorized by section 9730. 5) No timely election was filed under section 13502 to probate the decedent's property otherwise passing to the petitioner as the surviving spouse without administration (see section 13500). If the petitioner intends to request an order allowing a late election under section 13502(b), the petition must be amended to request that relief (or a separate ex parte petition must be filed). The election must include the statement required by LRSF 14.35(I)(5)("the statement must clearly state that he/she has been fully informed about the reasons for a probate (rather than, e.g., a Spousal Property Petition) and the consequences thereof, including potential delay and increased fees.") PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GREGSON 1996 TRUST, DATED JUNE 28, 1996
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motons And Notice Of Motion In Support Of Trustees Motion To Compel Further Response To Inspecton Demands Set One And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Ross C. Moody: Trustee John M. Gregson's Motion to Compel Further Responses from Anne-Marie O'Halloran to Trustee's Inspection Demands (set one) is GRANTED. Petitioner is ordered to separately address Inspection Demand Nos. 2, 17, 20, 26, 28, 30, 40, 46-48, 50, 52, and 54-64 by, identifying what documents Petitioner has in her possession, what documents (by Bates number) she is producing or has produced and what documents she is unable to locate after a good faith search. Said further responses are ordered to be served within ten (10) days of entry of this order. The request for sanctions is denied. (See, Karlsson v. Ford Motor Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1202, 1217 ["Discovery sanctions must be tailored in order to remedy the offending party's discovery abuse, should not give the aggrieved party more than what it is entitled to, and should not be used to punish the offending party."].) Counsel for trustee Mr. John M. Gregson shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SUSAN MARIKO KOBAYASHI AND CHARLES SUNGWON HAN REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED APRIL 29, 1999
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Modification And Amendment Of Trust
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required, as the following issues (e-mailed to counsel on 06/27/2023 - see also tentative ruling for 08/16/2023 hearing) remain unaddressed: 1) No proposed order has been received or lodged. 2) The trust date included in the petition caption and case title is incorrect. An ex parte petition for an order authorizing the clerk to correct the case title has not been processed. 3) Is the trust real property community property or either settlor's separate property? Please provide facts in support of the property characterization. 4) Is there any trust asset other than the real property? If so, please provide a brief description, value, and property characterization of each asset. 5) Is the date of birth of each child of the settlors stated in Article 1.4 of the trust true and correct? 6) Have both children of the settlors consented to the proposed elimination of the Bypass Trust? 7) Have Ann Kobayashi and Dale Kobayashi named in Article 7.4 of the trust consented to the proposed designation of the settlors' children as successor trustees of the Bypass Trust? It should be noted that the petitioner's power to designate successor trustees does not apply to the Bypass Trust under Article 7.3. 8) The petition must allege circumstances not known to and anticipated by the deceased settlor re her designation of successor trustees of the Bypass Trust in support of the modification under Probate Code section 15409. 9) Each Article of the trust sought to be modified must be set forth verbatim in the petition and proposed order. 10) Each Article of the trust sought to be deleted in its entirety must be identified as such in the petition and proposed order. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(C) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF BELEN PATRICIA BARRERA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/4/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] (3) No proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate has been filed yet. (Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.]) (4) No proof of publication of the notice has been filed yet. (5) The Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code Section 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially with a cover sheet. (6) Items 5a(3) or (4) and 5a(7) or (8) of the petition must be answered. (7) Item 8 must list all heirs of the decedent. Who is/was the decedent's father? He must receive or waive notice. If he is deceased, his date of death must be set forth. If he post-deceased, his successor(s)-in-interest must be identified and must receive notice. (8) If the father is living (or his successor(s)-in-interest if he post-deceased), he must also waive bond if the Court is to appoint without bond. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned examiner by 11/20/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNIE LUNG LAU
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 12/6/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Neither the mailed nor published notice indicated that the petitioner seeks to have a will admitted. (2) No declination by the named executors (Chi Wah Lung or Joseph Lung if the handwritten interlineation is, in fact, the testator's) has been filed yet. (3) The nominated executors are also missing from item 8 of the petition. (4) The will waives bond only for the persons nominated in the will. (5) The will is a pour-over will to the 2004 Annie Lung Lau Revocable Trust. Item 8 of the petition must identify the trustee of that trust. (6) If the petitioner is the trustee, all of the trust beneficiaries must also be listed and clearly identified as such and must receive notice. (7) Item 9 of the petition appears to be erroneous, as there are several pages attached. (8) The petitioner might also want to clarify for the Court why the will says the testator is not currently married, but item 8 indicates that Paul Lau is a spouse. Did they marry after the will was executed? Deadline is 11/27/23 for providing courtesy copies to the examiner of any papers filed to address these issues. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
